[Repo Assist] Perf: precompute entity name counts in renderEntities (O(N) vs O(N²)) (#1071)

* Perf: precompute entity name counts in renderEntities to avoid O(N²) list scanning

Previously, for each entity in the rendered list, the code scanned the
entire entities list to count duplicates (List.filter ... List.length).
With N entities this is O(N²). Replace with a single-pass List.countBy
precomputation outside the loop, reducing to O(N).

commit.message:
feat: add update-env workflow to update .env without full setup (#31)

## Problem
Updating the `.env` file on the server required running the full
`setup-server` workflow, which also installs packages, configures
Docker, firewall, etc.

## Solution
A lightweight manual workflow that only writes the `.env` file.

## Changes

### `.github/workflows/update-env.yml` (new)
- `workflow_dispatch` only — no automatic triggers
- Same `testing` / `production` environment input as `setup-server`
- Runs the Ansible playbook with `--tags env` → only executes the "Write
.env file" task
- No bootstrap/deploy step

### `ansible/playbooks/setup-server.yml`
- Added `tags: [env]` to the "Write .env file from secrets" task so it
can be targeted independently

## Usage
GitHub → Actions → **Update Server .env** → Run workflow → choose
environment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
PercentageView: fix xmark buttons — scope GlassEffectContainer per card

Previous attempt (ZStack inside a shared GlassEffectContainer) did not
work because GlassEffectContainer composites ALL its descendants into one
rendering pass, collapsing ZStack z-ordering within that scope.

Root cause identified: the outer GlassEffectContainer wrapped the entire
ScrollView, so even a ZStack above the glass card was still inside the
glass compositing context, rendering the button underneath the material.

Fix: remove the single full-screen GlassEffectContainer; each element
now uses its own GlassEffectContainer scoped to just that card. The
clear button lives in a ZStack OUTSIDE the card's GlassEffectContainer,
so it is genuinely above the glass layer in the render tree.

Also added previewRate/previewValue init params (default empty) to allow
rendering the filled state in previews for visual verification.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove useRuntimeConfig from module-level axios setup (#3)

## Problem

`api/index.ts` called `useRuntimeConfig()` at module initialisation time
via a `getApiBase()` helper. This runs before the Nuxt plugin context is
ready on the server, causing:

```
[Vue Router warn]: uncaught error during route navigation:
Error: [nuxt] instance unavailable
    at useNuxtApp
    at useRuntimeConfig
    at index-DV9GKTDj.mjs:39
```

## Fix

Remove `getApiBase()` entirely and reference
`process.env.NUXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE` directly in the `axios.create()`
call.

- On the **server** (SSR): the env var is available as a real process
environment variable.
- On the **client**: Nuxt/Vite inlines the value at build time (same
source as `runtimeConfig.public.apiBase` in `nuxt.config.ts`).

No behaviour change — the fallback `http://localhost:8080` is preserved.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
PercentageView: fix clear button hidden behind glass — use ZStack above GlassEffectContainer

Root cause:
GlassEffectContainer composites all children into a single glass
rendering pass. .overlay() on a card child was drawn within that
compositing context, so subsequent cards' glass surfaces rendered
on top of it — making the button visually and hit-test invisible.

Fix:
Introduced cardRow(field:text:) which wraps each inputCard in a
plain ZStack(alignment: .trailing). The clear button is placed as
the ZStack's top layer, completely outside the glass compositing
context, guaranteed to render above all glass surfaces.

inputCard: removed .overlay and Color.clear spacer; replaced with
a slim Color.clear(width:36) spacer in the HStack so text doesn't
run under the xmark position.

Button styling: white foreground + subtle black shadow so the icon
is clearly readable over any glass tint colour.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
PercentageView: fix xmark button — overlay above glass, clears all fields

Root cause — button not responding:
  The xmark button was rendered inside the HStack which then had
  .glassEffect() applied. The glass effect's hit-test surface covered
  the entire card area and consumed touches before they reached the
  button, making it silently unresponsive.

Fix:
  Moved xmark to .overlay(alignment: .trailing) placed AFTER the
  .glassEffect() modifier. Overlays render and hit-test on top of the
  glass surface, so taps always reach the button.

Behaviour change (per user request):
  Tapping the xmark on any field now calls clearAll() — all three
  fields are cleared at once rather than just the tapped field.

Also:
  - 44×44 pt hit target + .contentShape(Rectangle()) for reliable touch
  - Visibility condition uses any-field-non-empty so the xmark appears
    on all cards consistently as long as any input exists
  - Space reserved with Color.clear so card width stays stable

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
PercentageView: remove Calculate button, fix logic bugs, numeric-only input

Removed:
- 'Calculate' button — redundant, calculation already fires on every
  keystroke via onChange

Logic fix — reentrance bug:
- onChange was calling calculate() which set the solved field's text,
  triggering onChange again, which reset solvedField = nil → tint and
  result badge vanished immediately on every calculation
- Fix: guard focusedField == field in onChange — programmatic text
  updates (from calculate) are not focused, so they are skipped cleanly

Logic fix — locale-formatted string parsing:
- Percent.*ToString used .formatted() which produces locale-grouped
  strings ('1,234.00'). sanitize() turned that into '1.234.00' which
  Double() rejects, breaking recalculation after the first solve
- Fix: calculate() now uses String(format: '%g', result) directly —
  always parseable, no locale grouping
- parse() helper strips all non-digit/dot chars before Double() for
  robustness in both user input and computed output paths

Numeric-only input:
- numericOnly(_:) filters to digits + at most one decimal point;
  comma is replaced with dot (European decimal separator support)
- Illegal characters are rejected immediately on entry (onChange
  writes back the filtered value, triggering a second onChange
  which then recalculates cleanly)
- xmark clear button now also calls calculate() so sibling fields
  update when one field is cleared

UX:
- Clear button is now full-width and labelled 'Clear All'
- Deprecated NavigationView in #Preview → NavigationStack

0 warnings, 0 errors.

commit.message:
perf: pool WAL segment read handles via SafeFileHandle + RandomAccess (#1261)

#1240: Each record read previously opened and closed a new FileStream.
Under concurrent load (e.g., 50 users × queries touching 100 records
each = 5000 file opens), this created excessive OS overhead and risked
exhausting the file descriptor limit.

Fix:
- Cache SafeFileHandle per segment in ConcurrentDictionary
- Replace FileStream.Seek/Read with RandomAccess.Read (thread-safe,
  no locking needed — multiple threads can read different offsets
  from the same handle concurrently)
- Evict handles before segment deletion/replacement in compaction
- Dispose all cached handles in WalStore.Dispose()

Result: A query touching 1000 records from the same segment now
opens 1 file handle instead of 1000.

Co-authored-by: WillEastbury <WillEastbury@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Add API Key authentication for Elasticsearch connections (#343)

Dockit only supported Basic (username/password) auth for Elasticsearch.
This adds API Key authentication as an alternative, using the
`Authorization: ApiKey <key>` header per Elasticsearch's documented auth
method.

## Core changes

- **`requestUtil.ts`** — Unified `buildAuthHeader(authType, username,
password, apiKey?)` handles both Basic and API Key auth in a single
function; also fixes `undefined` credential normalization in Basic auth
encoding
- **`ElasticsearchConnection` type** — Added `authType?: 'basic' |
'apiKey'` and `apiKey?: string` fields; `apiKey` is persisted via the
same path as `password`
- **`fetchApi.ts`** — `fetchWrapper` and `loadHttpClient` now accept
`authType`/`apiKey`; delegates to unified `buildAuthHeader`
- **`connectionStore.ts`** — `queryToCurl` routes to the appropriate
auth header via unified `buildAuthHeader`

## UI

- Auth type tab switch (Basic / API Key) added to the ES connection
dialog — mirrors the DynamoDB local/cloud tab pattern
- API Key mode shows a single masked input for the pre-encoded key;
switching auth type clears irrelevant credentials and resets validation
state
- `authType` persists with the connection and is restored on edit;
legacy connections without a stored `authType` correctly default to
`'basic'`
- API Key field is required when API Key auth type is selected (Zod
`superRefine` validation)

## i18n

New keys in `enUS` and `zhCN`: `authenticationType`, `authTypeBasic`,
`authTypeApiKey`, `apiKey`, `apiKeyPlaceholder`,
`formValidation.apiKeyRequired`; 401 error message updated to be
auth-type-neutral

## Backward compatibility

Existing stored connections without `authType` fall through to Basic
auth — no migration needed.

## Example header produced

```
Authorization: ApiKey dGVzdC1pZDp0ZXN0LWtleQ==
```
